title: Mooring connector for connecting synthetic fibre ropes
abstract: The mooring connector 10 has a hollow body or shell (12) and a central core (14). The hollow body or shell (12) is formed from an engineering polymer, e.g. polyamide, and is generally cylindrical and has two shell parts (16, 18). The hollow body (12) has an outer surface (20) and an inner surface (22). The outer surface (20) has a central groove portion or rope running surface (24) and two outer groove portions or rope running surfaces (26, 28). The two outer groove portions or rope running surfaces (26, 28) are tapered, such that the outer groove portions (26, 28) are not equally spaced from the central groove portion (24) around the entire circumference of the hollow body or shell (12). The central core (14) is a solid body that is formed from a metallic material, for example steel. The central core (14) is generally cylindrical and has a shape that is configured to correspond to the shape of the recess (30) of the hollow body or shell (12). The central core (14) has a central portion (44) and arms (46, 48) that extend from the central portion (44) to outer flanges (50, 52). The diameter of the central core (14) at the outer flanges (50, 52) and the central portion (44) is greater than the diameter of the central core (14) at the arms (46, 48). This is to ensure that the central core (14) has the required strength in the areas of the mooring connector (10) that supports a rope, in use.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A mooring connector including a central core and a shell, wherein the shell includes a rope running surface. 2. A mooring connector according to claim 1, wherein the central core includes a first material and the shell includes a second material, wherein the first material is different to the second material . 3. A mooring connector according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the central core includes a metallic material and the shell includes a non- metallic material. 4. A mooring connector according to claim 1, claim 2 or claim 3, wherein the shell substantially surrounds the central core. 5. A mooring connector according to claim 1, claim 2, claim 3 or claim 4. wherein the shell has an inner surface that is arranged to receive the central core. 6. A mooring connector according to claim 5, wherein the inner surface includes a recess to receive the central core. 7. A mooring connector according to any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the shell has an outer surface on which the rope running surface is formed. 8. A mooring connector according to any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the shell includes a plurality of rope running surfaces. 9. A mooring connector according to claim 8, wherein at least one rope running surface of the plurality of rope running surfaces is tapered. 10. A mooring connector according to claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the shell includes a central groove in which a first rope running surface of the plurality of rope running surfaces is located. 11. A mooring connector according to claim 8, claim 9 or claim 10, wherein the shell includes an outer groove in which a second rope running surface of the plurality of rope running surfaces is located. 12. A mooring connector according to any of claims 1 to 11, wherein the shell includes a first shell part and a second shell part. 13. A mooring connector according to any of claims 1 to 12, the mooring connector further including an outer casing that is configured to substantially surround the shell. 14. A mooring connector according to claim 12 or claim 13, wherein the outer casing includes a recess that is configured to allow a rope to pass therethrough. 15. A mooring connector according to claim 14, wherein the outer casing includes a plurality of recesses that are configured to allow a rope to pass therethrough. 16. A mooring connector according to any of claims 12 to 15, wherein the outer casing includes a first casing part and a second casing part. 17. A mooring connector according to claim 16, when dependent on claim 15, in which the first casing part includes at least one recess of the plurality of recesses that are configured to allow a rope to pass therethrough and the second casing part includes a further at least one recess of the plurality of recesses that are configured to allow a rope to pass therethrough.
